title: Level 18.85
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 18.85: "I Spy"}}{{Class|p1=3|p2=3|p3=0}} '''Level 18.85''' is a sublevel between [[Level 18]] and [[Level 19]]. == Description == '''Level 18.85,''' also known as '''"I Spy"''', is a sprawling yet desolate metropolis constructed entirely of oversized physical children's toys from the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]. Such toys may include generic wooden blocks and tracks, Hot Wheels, dice, chess pieces, playing cards, yo-yos, trophies, teddy bears, golf balls, marbles and many more. Notably, the appearance of the level may change based on which toys the wanderer who observes it is most familiar with, similar to the shapeshifting properties of [[Level 18]] (with the exception of electronic toys such as Furbys, RC cars or the like). Action figures can also manifest here, resulting in an abundance of eerie, lifeless yet life-size figurines populating the region. The aforementioned toys can be arranged in a variety of structures, resembling towers, plazas, highways, tunnels, balconies and overlooks. Similar to the vast array of toys changing based on a wanderer's experience, the architectural style of structures can also vary (e.g. gothic, modern, asian), though the freeways are a constant between all variations. Some particularly interesting recorded structures have been telephone poles constructed with yo-yos, plazas out of puzzle pieces, and fountains made from tops. {{Blurimage|align=Right|caption=An artistic rendition of the sublevel.|file=Eye spy.jpg}}The sublevel has a day/night cycle, which lasts approximately 12 hours, half the length of a normal day. It has been confirmed that there is no time dilation factor, and that time flows at the same pace within '''Level 18.85''' as outside of it; the celestial bodies simply move quicker here.Though the presence of moving celestial bodies implies that this sublevel is a planet, no other evidence has been provided to support this theory. When night falls, the level is illuminated in a strange yellow glow without a discernible source that fades at daybreak, which aids travel during these hours. The moon, sun and stars can be seen during their respective times of the day, though they appear slightly off-kilter from the celestial bodies of the [[The Frontrooms|Frontrooms]]; the sun seems larger and brighter, and stars are not arranged in the same constellations. These differences have yet to be studied but are not priority, as they could simply be visual hallucinations or changes like the varying types of toys scattered around. '''Level 18.85''' is devoid of entities and mental hazards, but there is little food or water and exits are few and far between, so wanderers will need to travel fairly quickly to avoid running out of resources. The winding pathways and towering structures will quickly begin to blur together in one's perception. Additionally, all electronics seem to mysteriously disappear upon entry to '''Level 18.85''', such as cell phones, radios, and handheld cameras- hence the lack of photos. However, any devices that were lost when entering the sublevel will reappear when a wanderer exits. Devices that enter '''Level 18.85''' unaccompanied by a wanderer, or devices that are remotely piloted, like drones, are thought to disappear forever. == Weather == '''Level 18.85''''s temperature fluctuates around 65 degrees Fahrenheit, caused by the moderate to heavy winds whistling between the level's towering wooden structures. The winds have been known to both occasionally subside, resulting in an eerie silence, and increase, resulting in moderate to severe rain. During precipitation events, water can quickly build up in the bottom of the level, since it does not have any sort of drainage system, leading to flash flooding up to a recorded high of approximately 12 feet. The high waters can carry loose objects with them- which unfortunately happens to be most of the objects in '''Level 18.85'''. If water begins to rise above 5 feet in depth, the level's structure may become unstable and begin to collapse; in this case, even running for your life is unlikely to save you from the ensuing chain reaction. Once the precipitation has ceased, the water will soak into the surrounding blocks over the course of a few hours, and the bottom regions will be safe to navigate again. == Freeways & Platforms == One of the main features of '''Level 18.85''' are its expansive, winding wooden highways spreading nonsensically in every direction. They are the simplest form of travel around the sublevel due to their elevation, but their frequent twists and turns make them difficult to navigate, and wanderers may frequently find themselves moving in circles. Additionally, their narrow nature makes them precarious, and falling off of them can certainly lead to severe injuries if one is high enough above the ground. The many vehicles scattered around the level's suspended roadways, are, in fact, quite useless, since they are merely plastic and therefore not functional (in addition to the narrow roads and frequent traffic jams prohibiting their movement in general). The freeways occasionally converge in 2-4 lines at stations or platforms. These platforms also commonly connect to larger structures and staircases, serving as gateways to the lower areas and block tunnels. == Entities == This sublevel is entirely devoid of entities. == Colonies & Outposts == The effects of [[Phenomenon 15]] on '''Level 18.85''', along with the scarcity of resources and exits, have rendered it unable to be habituated for any substantial period of time. == Entrances == * Entering a building resembling a daycare in [[Level 11|Level 11.]] * Walking down a solid wood hallway in [[Level 27]]. * Climbing into a toy chest in [[Level 18]] will bring wanderers here. == Exits == * Noclipping into a chess piece can lead to [[Level 767]]. * Sinking to the bottom of a flash flood may lead to [[Level 7]]. * Climbing a particularly tall tower transports you to [[Level 11]]. * Entering a large building directly next to a platform will bring wanderers to [[Level 19]]. * Descend far enough into the wooden block tunnels to enter [[The Metro]]. * Attempting to [[Level DATA LOST|
[DATA LOST]
]] will send one to [[Level DATA LOST|
[DATA LOST]
